texts.orig: Writing the Programs
texts.text: Writing the Programs

texts.label: text
texts.orig: programming you have done for your classes. In class, your work is done independently, so that your instructor can judge its quality and suggest improvements. However, in the wider world, most software is developed by teams, and a variety of jobs are required to generate a quality product. Even when writing the code itself, many people are usually involved, and a great deal of cooperation and coordination is required. Thus, it is very important for others to be able to understand not only what you have written, but also why you have written it and how it fits in with their work.
texts.text: programming you have done for your classes. In class, your work is done independently, so that your instructor can judge its quality and suggest improvements. However, in the wider world, most software is developed by teams, and a variety of jobs are required to generate a quality product. Even when writing the code itself, many people are usually involved, and a great deal of cooperation and coordination is required. Thus, it is very important for others to be able to understand not only what you have written, but also why you have written it and how it fits in with their work.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: For these reasons, you must know your organization's standards and procedures before you begin to write code. Many companies insist that their code conform to style, format, and content standards, so that the code and associated documentation are clear to everyone who reads them. For example, Sidebar 7.1 describes Microsoft's approach to programming standards.
texts.text: For these reasons, you must know your organization's standards and procedures before you begin to write code. Many companies insist that their code conform to style, format, and content standards, so that the code and associated documentation are clear to everyone who reads them. For example, Sidebar 7.1 describes Microsoft's approach to programming standards.

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: Standards for You
texts.text: Standards for You

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Standards and procedures can help you to organize your thoughts and avoid mistakes. Some of the procedures involve methods of documenting your code so that it is clear and easy to follow. Such documentation allows you to leave and return to your work without losing track of what you had been doing. Standardized documentation also helps in locating faults and making changes, because it clarifies which sections of your program perform which functions.
texts.text: Standards and procedures can help you to organize your thoughts and avoid mistakes. Some of the procedures involve methods of documenting your code so that it is clear and easy to follow. Such documentation allows you to leave and return to your work without losing track of what you had been doing. Standardized documentation also helps in locating faults and making changes, because it clarifies which sections of your program perform which functions.

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: SIDEBAR 7.1 PROGRAMMING STANDARDS AT MICROSOFT
texts.text: SIDEBAR 7.1 PROGRAMMING STANDARDS AT MICROSOFT

texts.label: text
texts.orig: C usumano and Selby (1995, 1997) have studied software development at Microsoft. They point out that Microsoft tries to blend some aspects of software engineering practice into its software development cycle while preserving the creativity and individuality that hackers usually exhibit. Thus, Microsoft must find ways 'that structure and coordinate what the individual members do while allowing them enough flexibility to be creative and evolve the product's details in stages.' Because of market pressures and changing needs, Microsoft teams iterate among designing components, building them, and testing them. For example, team members revise both the types of features and their details as they learn more about what the product will do.
texts.text: C usumano and Selby (1995, 1997) have studied software development at Microsoft. They point out that Microsoft tries to blend some aspects of software engineering practice into its software development cycle while preserving the creativity and individuality that hackers usually exhibit. Thus, Microsoft must find ways 'that structure and coordinate what the individual members do while allowing them enough flexibility to be creative and evolve the product's details in stages.' Because of market pressures and changing needs, Microsoft teams iterate among designing components, building them, and testing them. For example, team members revise both the types of features and their details as they learn more about what the product will do.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: However, flexibility does not preclude standards. Nearly all of Microsoft's teams work at a single physical site, using common development languages (usually C and C++), common coding styles, and standard development tools.These standards help teams to communicate, to discuss the pros and cons of different design alternatives, and to resolve problems. Microsoft also requires its teams to collect a small set of measurements, including information about when failures occur and when the underlying faults are found and fixed.These measurements guide decisions about when to continue development and when to ship a product.
texts.text: However, flexibility does not preclude standards. Nearly all of Microsoft's teams work at a single physical site, using common development languages (usually C and C++), common coding styles, and standard development tools.These standards help teams to communicate, to discuss the pros and cons of different design alternatives, and to resolve problems. Microsoft also requires its teams to collect a small set of measurements, including information about when failures occur and when the underlying faults are found and fixed.These measurements guide decisions about when to continue development and when to ship a product.

texts.label: page_header
texts.orig: Section 7.1
texts.text: Section 7.1

texts.label: page_header
texts.orig: Programming Standards and Procedures
texts.text: Programming Standards and Procedures

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Standards and procedures also help in translating designs to code. By structuring code  according  to  standards, you  maintain  the  correspondence  between  design components and code components. Consequently, changes in design are easy to implement in the code. Similarly, modifications to code that result from changes in hardware or  interface  specifications  are  straightforward, and  the  possibility  of  error  is minimized.
texts.text: Standards and procedures also help in translating designs to code. By structuring code  according  to  standards, you  maintain  the  correspondence  between  design components and code components. Consequently, changes in design are easy to implement in the code. Similarly, modifications to code that result from changes in hardware or  interface  specifications  are  straightforward, and  the  possibility  of  error  is minimized.

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: Standards for Others
texts.text: Standards for Others

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Once your code is complete, others are likely to use it in a variety of ways. For example, as we shall see in later chapters, a separate team may test the code. Or another set of people may integrate your software with other programs to build and test subsystems and finally the whole system. Even after the system is up and running, changes may be needed, either because of a fault or because the customer wants to change the way the system performs its functions.You may not be part of those maintenance or test teams, so it is essential that you organize, format, and document your code to make it easy for others to understand what it does and how it works.
texts.text: Once your code is complete, others are likely to use it in a variety of ways. For example, as we shall see in later chapters, a separate team may test the code. Or another set of people may integrate your software with other programs to build and test subsystems and finally the whole system. Even after the system is up and running, changes may be needed, either because of a fault or because the customer wants to change the way the system performs its functions.You may not be part of those maintenance or test teams, so it is essential that you organize, format, and document your code to make it easy for others to understand what it does and how it works.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: For example, suppose every program produced by your company begins with a section  describing  the  program's  functions  and  interfaces  with  other  programs. The opening section might look like this:
texts.text: For example, suppose every program produced by your company begins with a section  describing  the  program's  functions  and  interfaces  with  other  programs. The opening section might look like this:

texts.label: code
texts.orig: ******************************************************* * * COMPONENT TO FIND INTERSECTION OF TWO LINES * * COMPONENT NAME: FINDPT * PROGRAMMER: E. ELLIS * VERSION: 1.0 (2 FEBRUARY 2001) * * PROCEDURE INVOCATION: *   CALL FINDPT (A1, B1, C1, A2, B2, C2, XS, YS, FLAG) * INPUT PARAMETERS: *   INPUT LINES ARE OF THE FORM *        A1*X + B1*Y + C1 = 0 AND *        A2*X + B2*Y + C2 = 0 *   SO INPUT IS COEFFICIENTS A1, B1, C1 AND A2, B2, C2 * OUTPUT PARAMETERS: *   IF LINES ARE PARALLEL, FLAG SET TO 1. *   ELSE  FLAG = 0 AND POINT OF INTERSECTION IS (XS, YS) * ******************************************************
texts.text: ******************************************************* * * COMPONENT TO FIND INTERSECTION OF TWO LINES * * COMPONENT NAME: FINDPT * PROGRAMMER: E. ELLIS * VERSION: 1.0 (2 FEBRUARY 2001) * * PROCEDURE INVOCATION: *   CALL FINDPT (A1, B1, C1, A2, B2, C2, XS, YS, FLAG) * INPUT PARAMETERS: *   INPUT LINES ARE OF THE FORM *        A1*X + B1*Y + C1 = 0 AND *        A2*X + B2*Y + C2 = 0 *   SO INPUT IS COEFFICIENTS A1, B1, C1 AND A2, B2, C2 * OUTPUT PARAMETERS: *   IF LINES ARE PARALLEL, FLAG SET TO 1. *   ELSE  FLAG = 0 AND POINT OF INTERSECTION IS (XS, YS) * ******************************************************

texts.orig: A maintenance programmer reading blocks like this one will find the component that needs to be changed more easily. Once that component is located, if the data names are clear and the interfaces well-defined, the maintenance programmer can be sure that the change needed will not have any unexpected effects on other parts of the code.
texts.text: A maintenance programmer reading blocks like this one will find the component that needs to be changed more easily. Once that component is located, if the data names are clear and the interfaces well-defined, the maintenance programmer can be sure that the change needed will not have any unexpected effects on other parts of the code.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Automated  tools  are  available  that  can  analyze  the  code  to  determine  which procedures are called by this component and which procedures invoke it. That is, the documentation generated by the tools points up to the components that may invoke it and down to those called by the procedure. With information such as this, making a change to the system is relatively straightforward.At the end of this chapter, we examine an example of standards and procedures to see how they may direct our programming efforts.
texts.text: Automated  tools  are  available  that  can  analyze  the  code  to  determine  which procedures are called by this component and which procedures invoke it. That is, the documentation generated by the tools points up to the components that may invoke it and down to those called by the procedure. With information such as this, making a change to the system is relatively straightforward.At the end of this chapter, we examine an example of standards and procedures to see how they may direct our programming efforts.

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: Matching Design with Implementation
texts.text: Matching Design with Implementation

texts.label: text
texts.orig: The most critical standard is the need for a direct correspondence between the program design components and the program code components.The entire design process is of little value if the design's modularity is not carried forward into the code. Design characteristics, such as low coupling, high cohesion, and well-defined interfaces, should also be program characteristics, so that the algorithms, functions, interfaces, and data structures can be traced easily from design to code and back again.
texts.text: The most critical standard is the need for a direct correspondence between the program design components and the program code components.The entire design process is of little value if the design's modularity is not carried forward into the code. Design characteristics, such as low coupling, high cohesion, and well-defined interfaces, should also be program characteristics, so that the algorithms, functions, interfaces, and data structures can be traced easily from design to code and back again.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Remember  that  the  system's  general  purpose  is  likely  to  remain  the  same throughout the software's lifetime, but its nature may change over time as customers identify enhancements and modifications. For example, suppose you are part of a team designing computer-aided displays for automobiles.The system you build will probably always be part of an automobile, but the menus and input devices may change, or new features may be added.These changes are made first to the high-level design and then are traced through lower design levels to the code that must be modified.Thus, the correspondence between design and code is essential. In later chapters, we will see that testing, maintenance, and configuration management are impossible without the links established by these standards.
texts.text: Remember  that  the  system's  general  purpose  is  likely  to  remain  the  same throughout the software's lifetime, but its nature may change over time as customers identify enhancements and modifications. For example, suppose you are part of a team designing computer-aided displays for automobiles.The system you build will probably always be part of an automobile, but the menus and input devices may change, or new features may be added.These changes are made first to the high-level design and then are traced through lower design levels to the code that must be modified.Thus, the correspondence between design and code is essential. In later chapters, we will see that testing, maintenance, and configuration management are impossible without the links established by these standards.

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: 7.2 PROGRAMMING GUIDELINES
texts.text: 7.2 PROGRAMMING GUIDELINES

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Programming involves a great deal of creativity. Remember that the design is a guide to the function or purpose of each component, but the programmer has great flexibility in implementing the design as code.The design or requirements specification may suggest a programming language, either directly because it is specified by the designers or customers, or indirectly because of the constructs used. Language-specific guidelines are not addressed here, because there are many good books on the subject. Instead, we discuss several guidelines that apply to programming in general, regardless of the language.
texts.text: Programming involves a great deal of creativity. Remember that the design is a guide to the function or purpose of each component, but the programmer has great flexibility in implementing the design as code.The design or requirements specification may suggest a programming language, either directly because it is specified by the designers or customers, or indirectly because of the constructs used. Language-specific guidelines are not addressed here, because there are many good books on the subject. Instead, we discuss several guidelines that apply to programming in general, regardless of the language.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: No matter what language is used, each program component involves at least three major  aspects: control  structures, algorithms, and  data  structures. We  examine  each more closely.
texts.text: No matter what language is used, each program component involves at least three major  aspects: control  structures, algorithms, and  data  structures. We  examine  each more closely.

texts.label: page_header
texts.orig: Section 7.2
texts.text: Section 7.2

texts.label: page_header
texts.orig: Programming Guidelines
texts.text: Programming Guidelines

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: Control Structures
texts.text: Control Structures

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Many of the control structures for a component are suggested by the architecture and design, and we want to preserve them as the design is translated to code. In the case of some architectures, such as implicit invocation and object-oriented design, control is based on system states and changes in variables. In other, more procedural designs, control depends on the structure of the code itself. For any type of design, it is important for your program structure to reflect the design's control structure. Readers should not have to jump wildly through the code, marking sections to which to return and wondering whether they have followed the right path. They should concentrate on what is being done by the program, not on the control flow.Thus, many guidelines and standards suggest that the code be written so that you can read a component easily from the top down.
texts.text: Many of the control structures for a component are suggested by the architecture and design, and we want to preserve them as the design is translated to code. In the case of some architectures, such as implicit invocation and object-oriented design, control is based on system states and changes in variables. In other, more procedural designs, control depends on the structure of the code itself. For any type of design, it is important for your program structure to reflect the design's control structure. Readers should not have to jump wildly through the code, marking sections to which to return and wondering whether they have followed the right path. They should concentrate on what is being done by the program, not on the control flow.Thus, many guidelines and standards suggest that the code be written so that you can read a component easily from the top down.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Let us look at an example to see how restructuring can aid understanding. Consider the following program. Its control skips around among the program's statements, making it difficult to follow.
texts.text: Let us look at an example to see how restructuring can aid understanding. Consider the following program. Its control skips around among the program's statements, making it difficult to follow.

texts.label: code
texts.orig: benefit = minimum; if (age < 75) goto A; benefit = maximum; goto C; if (age < 65) goto B; if (age < 55) goto C; A:    if (age < 65) goto B; benefit = benefit * 1.5 + bonus; goto C; B:   if (age < 55) goto C; benefit = benefit * 1.5; C:    next statement
texts.text: benefit = minimum; if (age < 75) goto A; benefit = maximum; goto C; if (age < 65) goto B; if (age < 55) goto C; A:    if (age < 65) goto B; benefit = benefit * 1.5 + bonus; goto C; B:   if (age < 55) goto C; benefit = benefit * 1.5; C:    next statement

texts.label: text
texts.orig: We can accomplish the same thing in a format that is easier to follow by rearranging the code:
texts.text: We can accomplish the same thing in a format that is easier to follow by rearranging the code:

texts.label: code
texts.orig: if (age < 55) benefit = minimum; elseif (age < 65) benefit = minimum + bonus; elseif (age < 75) benefit = minimum * 1.5 + bonus; else benefit = maximum;
texts.text: if (age < 55) benefit = minimum; elseif (age < 65) benefit = minimum + bonus; elseif (age < 75) benefit = minimum * 1.5 + bonus; else benefit = maximum;

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Of course, it is not always possible or practical to have exactly a top-down flow. For example, reaching the end of a loop may disrupt the flow. However, it is helpful whenever possible to have the required action follow soon after the decision that generates it.
texts.text: Of course, it is not always possible or practical to have exactly a top-down flow. For example, reaching the end of a loop may disrupt the flow. However, it is helpful whenever possible to have the required action follow soon after the decision that generates it.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: We saw in previous chapters that modularity was a good design attribute. Its same advantages carry through to the code as well.By building a program from modular blocks, we can hide implementation details at different levels, making the entire system easier to understand, test, and maintain. In other words, we can consider a program component itself to be modular, and we can use macros, procedures, subroutines, methods, and inheritance to hide details while enhancing understandability. Moreover, the more modular the code component, the more easily it can be maintained and reused in other applications; modification can be isolated to a particular macro, subroutine, or other subcomponent.
texts.text: We saw in previous chapters that modularity was a good design attribute. Its same advantages carry through to the code as well.By building a program from modular blocks, we can hide implementation details at different levels, making the entire system easier to understand, test, and maintain. In other words, we can consider a program component itself to be modular, and we can use macros, procedures, subroutines, methods, and inheritance to hide details while enhancing understandability. Moreover, the more modular the code component, the more easily it can be maintained and reused in other applications; modification can be isolated to a particular macro, subroutine, or other subcomponent.

texts.label: page_header
texts.orig: Writing the Programs
texts.text: Writing the Programs

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Thus, in writing your code, keep in mind that generality is a virtue; do not make your code more specialized than it needs to be. For instance, a component that searches a string of 80 characters of text for a period can be written so that the input parameters include the length of the string and the character to be found.Then, the component can be used again to search a string of any length for any character. At the same time, do not  make  your  components  so  general  that  performance  and  understanding  are affected.
texts.text: Thus, in writing your code, keep in mind that generality is a virtue; do not make your code more specialized than it needs to be. For instance, a component that searches a string of 80 characters of text for a period can be written so that the input parameters include the length of the string and the character to be found.Then, the component can be used again to search a string of any length for any character. At the same time, do not  make  your  components  so  general  that  performance  and  understanding  are affected.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Other design characteristics translate to code components, such as coupling and cohesion.When you write your programs, remember to use parameter names and comments to exhibit the coupling among components. For instance, suppose you are writing a component to estimate income tax. It uses the values of gross income and deductions provided by other components. Instead of commenting your code with
texts.text: Other design characteristics translate to code components, such as coupling and cohesion.When you write your programs, remember to use parameter names and comments to exhibit the coupling among components. For instance, suppose you are writing a component to estimate income tax. It uses the values of gross income and deductions provided by other components. Instead of commenting your code with

texts.orig: Reestimate TAX
texts.text: Reestimate TAX

texts.label: text
texts.orig: it is better to write
texts.text: it is better to write

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Reestimate TAX based on values of GROSS_INC and DEDUCTS
texts.text: Reestimate TAX based on values of GROSS_INC and DEDUCTS

texts.label: text
texts.orig: The  second  comment  explains  how  the  calculation  is  tied  to  data  items  in  other components.
texts.text: The  second  comment  explains  how  the  calculation  is  tied  to  data  items  in  other components.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Your code must enable the reader to discern which parameters, if any, are being passed to the component and back again. Otherwise, testing and maintenance will be extremely difficult. In other words, dependence among components must be visible. By the same token, just as the system components were designed to hide information from one another, the subcomponents of your program should hide calculation details from each  other. For  example, in  the  earlier  string-searching  program, the  text-searching component must contain information about how the specified character is sought. But the calling components need not know how the character is found, only that it is found and where it is. This information hiding allows you to change the searching algorithm without disturbing the rest of the code.
texts.text: Your code must enable the reader to discern which parameters, if any, are being passed to the component and back again. Otherwise, testing and maintenance will be extremely difficult. In other words, dependence among components must be visible. By the same token, just as the system components were designed to hide information from one another, the subcomponents of your program should hide calculation details from each  other. For  example, in  the  earlier  string-searching  program, the  text-searching component must contain information about how the specified character is sought. But the calling components need not know how the character is found, only that it is found and where it is. This information hiding allows you to change the searching algorithm without disturbing the rest of the code.

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: Algorithms
texts.text: Algorithms

texts.label: text
texts.orig: The program design often specifies a class of algorithms to be used in coding the component you are writing. For example, the design may tell you to use a Quicksort, or it may list the logical steps of the Quicksort algorithm. However, you have a great deal of flexibility in converting the algorithm to code, subject to the constraints of the implementation language and hardware.
texts.text: The program design often specifies a class of algorithms to be used in coding the component you are writing. For example, the design may tell you to use a Quicksort, or it may list the logical steps of the Quicksort algorithm. However, you have a great deal of flexibility in converting the algorithm to code, subject to the constraints of the implementation language and hardware.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: One of the areas in which you have great discretion is the performance or efficiency of your implementation.Your instinct may tell you to make the code run as fast as possible. However, making the code faster may involve hidden costs:
texts.text: One of the areas in which you have great discretion is the performance or efficiency of your implementation.Your instinct may tell you to make the code run as fast as possible. However, making the code faster may involve hidden costs:

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: · the cost to write the faster code, which may be more complex and thus take more time to write
texts.text: the cost to write the faster code, which may be more complex and thus take more time to write

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: · the cost of time to test the code, whose complexity requires more test cases or test data
texts.text: the cost of time to test the code, whose complexity requires more test cases or test data

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: · the cost of time for users to understand the code
texts.text: the cost of time for users to understand the code

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: · the costs of time to modify the code, if necessary
texts.text: the costs of time to modify the code, if necessary

texts.orig: Section 7.2
texts.text: Section 7.2

texts.label: page_header
texts.orig: Programming Guidelines
texts.text: Programming Guidelines

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Thus, execution time is only a small part of the overall cost equation.You must balance execution time considerations with design quality, standards, and  customer  requirements. In particular, do not sacrifice clarity and correctness for speed.
texts.text: Thus, execution time is only a small part of the overall cost equation.You must balance execution time considerations with design quality, standards, and  customer  requirements. In particular, do not sacrifice clarity and correctness for speed.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: If speed is important to your implementation, you must learn how your compiler optimizes your code. Otherwise, the optimization may take your seemingly faster code and actually slow it down. To see how this paradoxical situation can happen, suppose you are writing code to implement a three-dimensional array. You decide to increase efficiency by creating instead a one-dimensional array and performing all the indexing computations yourself.Thus, your code computes such variables as
texts.text: If speed is important to your implementation, you must learn how your compiler optimizes your code. Otherwise, the optimization may take your seemingly faster code and actually slow it down. To see how this paradoxical situation can happen, suppose you are writing code to implement a three-dimensional array. You decide to increase efficiency by creating instead a one-dimensional array and performing all the indexing computations yourself.Thus, your code computes such variables as

texts.label: formula
texts.orig: index = 3*i + 2*j + k;

texts.label: text
texts.orig: to calculate the position of an entry in a three-dimensional array. However, your compiler may perform its array indexing in the registers, so execution time is small. If the compiler uses an additive increment technique in the registers, rather than adding and multiplying for each position calculation, then your one-dimensional array technique may actually result in increased execution time!
texts.text: to calculate the position of an entry in a three-dimensional array. However, your compiler may perform its array indexing in the registers, so execution time is small. If the compiler uses an additive increment technique in the registers, rather than adding and multiplying for each position calculation, then your one-dimensional array technique may actually result in increased execution time!

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: Data Structures
texts.text: Data Structures

texts.label: text
texts.orig: In writing your programs, you should format and store data so that data management and manipulation are straightforward. There are several techniques that use the structure of the data to suggest how the program should be organized.
texts.text: In writing your programs, you should format and store data so that data management and manipulation are straightforward. There are several techniques that use the structure of the data to suggest how the program should be organized.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Keeping the Program Simple. The program's design may specify some of the data structures to be used in implementing functions. Often, these structures are chosen because they fit into an overall scheme that promotes information hiding and control of component  interfaces. Data  manipulation  within  a  component  can  influence  your choice of data structures in a similar way. For example, restructuring data can simplify a program's calculations.To see how, suppose you are writing a program to determine the amount  of  federal  income  tax  due. As  input, you  are  given  the  amount  of  taxable income and are told the following:

Often, these structures are chosen because they fit into an overall scheme that promotes information hiding and control of component  interfaces. Data  manipulation  within  a  component  can  influence  your choice of data structures in a similar way. For example, restructuring data can simplify a program's calculations.To see how, suppose you are writing a program to determine the amount  of  federal  income  tax  due. As  input, you  are  given  the  amount  of  taxable income and are told the following:

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: 1. For the first $10,000 of income, the tax is 10%.
texts.text: For the first $10,000 of income, the tax is 10%.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: 2. For the next $10,000 of income above $10,000, the tax is 12%.
texts.text: For the next $10,000 of income above $10,000, the tax is 12%.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: 3. For the next $10,000 of income above $20,000, the tax is 15%.
texts.text: For the next $10,000 of income above $20,000, the tax is 15%.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: 4. For the next $10,000 of income above $30,000, the tax is 18%.
texts.text: For the next $10,000 of income above $30,000, the tax is 18%.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: 5. For any income above $40,000, the tax is 20%.
texts.text: For any income above $40,000, the tax is 20%.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Thus, someone who has a taxable income of $35,000 pays 10% of the first $10,000 (or $1000), 12% of the next $10,000 (or $1200), 15% of the next $10,000 (or $1500), and 18% of the remaining $5000 (or $900), for a total of $4600. To calculate the tax, you can include code in your component that reads in the taxable income and follows this algorithm:
texts.text: Thus, someone who has a taxable income of $35,000 pays 10% of the first $10,000 (or $1000), 12% of the next $10,000 (or $1200), 15% of the next $10,000 (or $1500), and 18% of the remaining $5000 (or $900), for a total of $4600. To calculate the tax, you can include code in your component that reads in the taxable income and follows this algorithm:

texts.label: code
texts.orig: tax = 0. if (taxable_income == 0) goto EXIT; if (taxable_income > 10000) tax = tax + 1000;
texts.text: tax = 0. if (taxable_income == 0) goto EXIT; if (taxable_income > 10000) tax = tax + 1000;

texts.orig: However, we can define a tax table  for  each 'bracket' of  tax  liability, as  shown  in Table 7.1, where we use a base figure and a percentage for each bracket.
texts.text: However, we can define a tax table  for  each 'bracket' of  tax  liability, as  shown  in Table 7.1, where we use a base figure and a percentage for each bracket.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Then, using the table, we can simplify our algorithm considerably:
texts.text: Then, using the table, we can simplify our algorithm considerably:

texts.label: code
texts.orig: for (int i-2; level=1; i <= 5; i++) if (taxable_income > bracket[i]) level = level + 1; tax = base[level]+percent[level]*(taxable_income-bracket[level]);
texts.text: for (int i-2; level=1; i <= 5; i++) if (taxable_income > bracket[i]) level = level + 1; tax = base[level]+percent[level]*(taxable_income-bracket[level]);

texts.orig: Notice  how  the  calculations  are  simplified  just  by  changing  the  way  the  data  are defined.This simplification makes the program easier to understand, test, and modify.
texts.text: Notice  how  the  calculations  are  simplified  just  by  changing  the  way  the  data  are defined.This simplification makes the program easier to understand, test, and modify.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Using  a  Data  Structure  to  Determine  a  Program  Structure. In  the  tax  table example, the way we define the data dictates how we perform the necessary calculations. In general, data structures can influence the organization and flow of a program. In  some  cases, the  data  structures  can  influence  the  choice  of  language, too. For

texts.label: text
texts.orig: example, LISP is designed to be a list processor, and it contains structures that make it much more attractive than some other languages for handling lists. Similarly, Ada and Eiffel contain constructs for handling unacceptable states called exceptions.
texts.text: example, LISP is designed to be a list processor, and it contains structures that make it much more attractive than some other languages for handling lists. Similarly, Ada and Eiffel contain constructs for handling unacceptable states called exceptions.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: A data structure is said to be recursive if it is defined by identifying an initial element  and  then  generating  successive  elements  as  a  function  of  those  previously defined. For example, a rooted tree is a graph composed of nodes and lines so that the following conditions hold:
texts.text: A data structure is said to be recursive if it is defined by identifying an initial element  and  then  generating  successive  elements  as  a  function  of  those  previously defined. For example, a rooted tree is a graph composed of nodes and lines so that the following conditions hold:

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: 1. Exactly one node of the tree is designated as the root.
texts.text: Exactly one node of the tree is designated as the root.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: 2. If the lines emanating from the root are erased, the resulting graph is a set of nonintersecting graphs, each of which is a rooted tree.
texts.text: If the lines emanating from the root are erased, the resulting graph is a set of nonintersecting graphs, each of which is a rooted tree.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Figure 7.1 illustrates a rooted tree, and Figure 7.2 shows how removing the root results in a set of smaller rooted trees.The root of each smaller tree is the node that had previously been connected to the original root of the larger tree.Thus, the rooted tree is defined in terms of its root and subtrees: a recursive definition.
texts.text: Figure 7.1 illustrates a rooted tree, and Figure 7.2 shows how removing the root results in a set of smaller rooted trees.The root of each smaller tree is the node that had previously been connected to the original root of the larger tree.Thus, the rooted tree is defined in terms of its root and subtrees: a recursive definition.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Programming languages such as Pascal allow recursive procedures to deal with recursive data structures. You may prefer to use recursive procedures for this type of data, since their use forces the burden of managing the data structure to be borne by the compiler rather than your program.The use of recursion may make the actual programming easier or may make your program more understandable. Thus, in general, you should consider the data structures carefully when deciding which language to use in implementing the design.
texts.text: Programming languages such as Pascal allow recursive procedures to deal with recursive data structures. You may prefer to use recursive procedures for this type of data, since their use forces the burden of managing the data structure to be borne by the compiler rather than your program.The use of recursion may make the actual programming easier or may make your program more understandable. Thus, in general, you should consider the data structures carefully when deciding which language to use in implementing the design.

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: General Guidelines
texts.text: General Guidelines

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Several overall strategies are useful in preserving the design quality in your code.
texts.text: Several overall strategies are useful in preserving the design quality in your code.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Localizing Input and Output. Those parts of a program that read input or generate output are highly specialized and must reflect characteristics of the underlying hardware and software. Because of this dependence, the program sections performing input and output functions are sometimes difficult to test. In fact, they may be the sections  most  likely  to  change  if  the  hardware  or  software  is  modified. Therefore, it  is desirable to localize these sections in components separate from the rest of the code.
texts.text: Localizing Input and Output. Those parts of a program that read input or generate output are highly specialized and must reflect characteristics of the underlying hardware and software. Because of this dependence, the program sections performing input and output functions are sometimes difficult to test. In fact, they may be the sections  most  likely  to  change  if  the  hardware  or  software  is  modified. Therefore, it  is desirable to localize these sections in components separate from the rest of the code.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: An added benefit of localization is generalization of the overall system. Other systemwide  functions  to  be  performed  on  the  input  (such  as  reformatting  or  type checking) can be included in the specialized component, relieving the other components of the burden and thus eliminating repetition. Similarly, putting output functions in one place makes the system easier to understand and change.
texts.text: An added benefit of localization is generalization of the overall system. Other systemwide  functions  to  be  performed  on  the  input  (such  as  reformatting  or  type checking) can be included in the specialized component, relieving the other components of the burden and thus eliminating repetition. Similarly, putting output functions in one place makes the system easier to understand and change.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Including Pseudocode. The design usually lays out a framework for each program  component. Then, you  add  your  creativity  and  expertise  to  build  the  lines  of code that implement the design. For example, the design may be relatively languageindependent, giving you many choices about the particular language constructs to use, how to use them, how the data will be represented, and so on. Since the design is an outline of what is to be done in a program component, it is useful to move in stages from the specified design to the code, rather than to translate the design immediately into code.
texts.text: Including Pseudocode. The design usually lays out a framework for each program  component. Then, you  add  your  creativity  and  expertise  to  build  the  lines  of code that implement the design. For example, the design may be relatively languageindependent, giving you many choices about the particular language constructs to use, how to use them, how the data will be represented, and so on. Since the design is an outline of what is to be done in a program component, it is useful to move in stages from the specified design to the code, rather than to translate the design immediately into code.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Pseudocode can be used to adapt the design to your chosen language. By adopting constructs and data representations without becoming involved immediately in the specifics of each command, you can experiment and decide which implementation is most desirable. In this way, code can be rearranged and restructured with a minimum of rewriting. For instance, suppose the design for a component of a text processing system states
